originalTitle: Oil prices fall after U.S. Energy secretary says Hormuz ship traffic is increasing
summary: President Donald Trump has repeatedly said a deal with Tehran to reopen Hormuz is close, but such an agreement still has not materialized.
content:
Oil prices dropped Tuesday after U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright said ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is "rising very meaningfully."

U.S. crude oil futures declined 3.4% to close at $88.20 per barrel. Brent futures, the international benchmark, lost 2.97% to settle at $91.45.

Wright did not provide specific data on how much oil flows through Hormuz have increased. He made the remarks in an interview with CNBC's Brian Sullivan at the Atlantic Council Global Energy Forum.

Wright said oil exports through Hormuz are rising and "will continue to rise."

Oil prices were down even after President Donald Trump accused Iran of shooting down a U.S. Apache helicopter that was patrolling Hormuz. The two pilots are safe and uninjured but the U.S. must respond to the attack, Trump said in a social media post .

More oil may be going through Hormuz than is publicly visible, JPMorgan analysts wrote in a June 4 note. The U.S. Navy has quietly coordinated with some ships that are trying to exit the Persian Gulf.

Some 2 million barrels per day might be getting out on tankers that have switched off their transponders, according to the bank's estimates.

"Despite the ongoing naval blockade and the steep decline in commercial traffic, surprising volumes of crude and petroleum products still appear to be transiting the Strait," the JPMorgan analysts said.

Meanwhile, Trump sought to convince the market Monday that a deal with Tehran to reopen Hormuz was "two or three days away," despite the outbreak of violence between Israel and Iran this week.

Trump has repeatedly said a deal with Tehran to reopen Hormuz is close, but such an agreement still has not materialized. The fragile ceasefire implemented in April nearly unraveled this week after Iran launched missiles at Israel in retaliation for its strikes in Lebanon.

Israel hit back with strikes on the Islamic Republic. Trump pressured Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to refrain from further attacks.

The violence spiked oil prices briefly Monday, but the volley of strikes appears to have ended without further escalation for now. Iran and Israel said they have ceased fire.

Oil prices have surged about 30% since the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran on Feb. 28. Tehran retaliated by attacking tankers in the Strait of Hormuz and mining the sea lane. Traffic through Hormuz has plunged as a consequence, triggering the biggest oil supply disruption in history.

Trump has sought to pressure Iran into a deal by imposing a naval blockade on its ports and vessels.

Oil industry executives and analysts say crude prices have remained moderate in comparison to the scale of the disruption due to the buffer provided by global stockpiles. But prices will likely spike later this year as those inventories rapidly decline at the same time summer demand hits its peak, they say.
chineseTitle: 美国能源部长表示霍尔木兹海峡船舶交通量增加后油价下跌
chineseSummary: 唐纳德·特朗普总统多次表示，与德黑兰就重新开放霍尔木兹海峡的协议已经接近，但这样的协议尚未实现。
chineseContent:
美国能源部长克里斯·赖特表示，通过霍尔木兹海峡的船舶交通“显着增加”后，周二油价下跌。

美国原油期货下跌 3.4%，收于每桶 88.20 美元。国际基准布伦特原油期货下跌 2.97%，收于 91.45 美元。

赖特没有提供有关流经霍尔木兹的石油流量增加了多少的具体数据。他是在大西洋理事会全球能源论坛上接受 CNBC 布莱恩·沙利文采访时发表上述言论的。

赖特表示，通过霍尔木兹海峡的石油出口正在增加，并且“将继续增加”。

即使唐纳德·特朗普总统指责伊朗击落一架正在霍尔木兹巡逻的美国阿帕奇直升机，油价仍然下跌。特朗普在社交媒体帖子中表示，这两名飞行员安全且没有受伤，但美国必须对此次袭击做出回应。

摩根大通分析师在 6 月 4 日的一份报告中写道，通过霍尔木兹海峡的石油数量可能比公开可见的还要多。美国海军已悄悄与一些试图离开波斯湾的船只进行协调。

据该银行估计，关闭应答器的油轮每天可能会输出约 200 万桶石油。

摩根大通分析师表示：“尽管海上封锁仍在持续，商业运输量急剧下降，但仍有大量原油和石油产品通过该海峡。”

与此同时，特朗普周一试图让市场相信，尽管以色列和伊朗之间本周爆发了暴力事件，但与德黑兰就重新开放霍尔木兹海峡的协议“还需要两三天”。

特朗普一再表示与德黑兰就重新开放霍尔木兹海峡的协议已接近达成，但这样的协议仍未实现。本周，伊朗向以色列发射导弹以报复其对黎巴嫩的袭击，四月份实施的脆弱停火几乎破裂。

以色列对伊斯兰共和国进行了反击。特朗普向以色列总理本杰明·内塔尼亚胡施压，要求其不要发动进一步袭击。

周一的暴力事件导致油价短暂飙升，但目前一系列罢工似乎已经结束，没有进一步升级。伊朗和以色列表示已经停火。

自2月28日美国和以色列袭击伊朗以来，油价已飙升约30%。德黑兰通过袭击霍尔木兹海峡的油轮并在海上航道布雷进行报复。结果，霍尔木兹海峡的交通量大幅下降，引发了历史上最严重的石油供应中断。

特朗普试图通过对伊朗港口和船只实施海上封锁来迫使伊朗达成协议。

石油行业高管和分析师表示，由于全球库存提供了缓冲，与中断的规模相比，原油价格仍然温和。但他们表示，今年晚些时候价格可能会飙升，因为这些库存在夏季需求达到顶峰的同时迅速下降。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
